title: Alexis Wangmene

(born March 1, 1989) is a Cameroonian professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") player who last played for [FAP](/wiki/FAP_Basketball "FAP Basketball") of the [Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League") (BAL). He played [college basketball](/wiki/College_basketball "College basketball") for the [Texas Longhorns](/wiki/Texas_Longhorns_men%27s_basketball "Texas Longhorns men's basketball") and has represented the [Cameroon national team](/wiki/Cameroon_men%27s_national_basketball_team "Cameroon men's national basketball team").

**Alexis Mang-Ikri Wangmene** (born March 1, 1989) is a Cameroonian professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") player who last played for [FAP](/wiki/FAP_Basketball "FAP Basketball") of the [Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League") (BAL). He played [college basketball](/wiki/College_basketball "College basketball") for the [Texas Longhorns](/wiki/Texas_Longhorns_men%27s_basketball "Texas Longhorns men's basketball") and has represented the [Cameroon national team](/wiki/Cameroon_men%27s_national_basketball_team "Cameroon men's national basketball team").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexis_Wangmene&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Wangmene was spotted by [San Antonio Spurs](/wiki/San_Antonio_Spurs "San Antonio Spurs") general manager [R. C. Buford](/wiki/R._C._Buford "R. C. Buford") at a *[Basketball Without Borders](/wiki/Basketball_Without_Borders "Basketball Without Borders")* camp in his native Cameroon. Buford and his wife brought Wangmene to the United States and resettled him in [San Antonio](/wiki/San_Antonio,_Texas "San Antonio, Texas"), where he played basketball and attended [Central Catholic Marianist High School](/wiki/Central_Catholic_Marianist_High_School "Central Catholic Marianist High School") for two years. During his junior year at Central, the power forward was named to the [TAPPS 6A All-State team](/wiki/Texas_Association_of_Private_and_Parochial_Schools "Texas Association of Private and Parochial Schools"). Following his junior year, Wangmene transferred to [Blair Academy](/wiki/Blair_Academy "Blair Academy") in [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"), from which he graduated in 2007.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## College career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexis_Wangmene&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: College career")]

At [Texas](/wiki/Texas_Longhorns_men%27s_basketball "Texas Longhorns men's basketball"), Wangmene missed all but four games of his sophomore year with a knee injury. In May 2009, he received a medical redshirt because of the injury and had three years of eligibility remaining prior to the 2009–10 season.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexis_Wangmene&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Professional career")]

After going undrafted in the [2012 NBA draft](/wiki/2012_NBA_draft "2012 NBA draft"), Wangmene joined the [San Antonio Spurs](/wiki/San_Antonio_Spurs "San Antonio Spurs") for the [2012 NBA Summer League](/wiki/2012_NBA_Summer_League "2012 NBA Summer League"). On November 2, 2012, he was selected by the [Austin Toros](/wiki/Austin_Toros "Austin Toros") in the fifth round of the [2012 NBA Development League Draft](/wiki/2012_NBA_Development_League_Draft "2012 NBA Development League Draft").[[3]](#cite_note-3) In July 2013, he re-joined the San Antonio Spurs for the [2013 NBA Summer League](/wiki/2013_NBA_Summer_League "2013 NBA Summer League").

In October 2013, Wangmene signed with Slovenian team [Hopsi Polzela](/wiki/KD_Hopsi_Polzela "KD Hopsi Polzela") for the 2013–14 season.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In June 2014, Wangmene signed a two-year deal with fellow Slovenian team [Krka Novo Mesto](/wiki/KK_Krka "KK Krka").[[5]](#cite_note-5) He was released by Krka in July 2015 following the 2014–15 season.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In November 2015, Wangmene with Polish team [Stal Ostrów Wielkopolski](/wiki/Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski "Stal Ostrów Wielkopolski").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

On October 30, 2016, Wangmene was selected by the [Austin Spurs](/wiki/Austin_Spurs "Austin Spurs") in the sixth round of the 2016 NBA Development League Draft.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Wangmene signed a contract with [Limburg United](/wiki/Limburg_United "Limburg United") of the Belgian league on January 29, 2020.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On October 23, 2021, Wangmene was selected by the [Austin Spurs](/wiki/Austin_Spurs "Austin Spurs") in the second round of the [2021 NBA G League Draft](/wiki/2021_NBA_G_League_draft_(October) "2021 NBA G League draft (October)") with their 39th overall pick.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He was then later waived on January 31, 2022.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In April 2022, Wangmene was revealed to be on the roster of Cameroon's [FAP](/wiki/FAP_Basketball "FAP Basketball") roster for the [2022 BAL season](/wiki/2022_BAL_season "2022 BAL season").[[13]](#cite_note-13)
